plan_summary: Baseline logistic regression model for Titanic survival binary classification with stratified 80/20 split
reviewer_reasoning: Iteration 1 establishes a solid baseline (AUC 0.835) with no risk flags, low overfitting, and no leakage. However, only one model family has been tried, 9 iterations remain, and tree-based models typically yield meaningful improvement on this dataset. Clear headroom exists.
router_reasoning: Natural progression from linear baseline to tree-based models. Logistic regression has limited capacity to capture non-linear interactions (e.g., Pclass x Fare, Age x Sex). Pivoting to gradient-boosted trees or random forest is the highest-value next step.
